revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:飯神社]]
<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py-->
{{Expand Japanese|飯神社}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Ii Shrine (Q11666643)
| native_name = 飯神社
| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15
| mapframe-wikidata = yes
| mapframe-point = none
| map_type = Japan
| map_caption = Ii Shrine (Q11666643)
| coordinates = {{coord|34.269921|133.840264}}
| map_relief = 1
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| deity = {{ill|Iiyorihiko|lt=Iiyorihiko|ja|飯依比古|qid=Q65292980|1=Iiyorihiko}}
}}

{{nihongo|'''Ii Shrine'''|飯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sanuki Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}
* {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}}
* {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}
* {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}<ref>Kokugakuin University Shrine database</ref>
* {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}}

== part of (P361) ==

* {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11632546|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province}}
** Series ordinal: 11
** P155: {{ill|Q11426120|ltq=Q11426120|qid=Q11426120|1=Kiyama Shrine}}
** P156: {{ill|Uhei Shrine|lt=Uhei Shrine|qid=Q135041389|1=Uhei Shrine}}

[[JA:飯神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q11666643}}

== Japanese content ==
{{Source clarification|date=January 2011}}
{{Shrine
|Name= Iii Shrine
|Image= {{Image needed|cat=Marugame City}}
|Location= {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|qid=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Marugame City|Q460835|qid=Q460835|1=Marugame, Kagawa}} Iino-chō Higashi 2-aza Yamane 20
|Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deities= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iii-hiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}
|Shrine rank= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}・Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
|Founded= Unknown
|Main hall style= {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
|Other name= 
|Pilgrimage site= 
|Annual festival= Fourth Sunday of October
|Rituals= 
|Map= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Iii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Iii Shrine''' (いいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Marugame City|Q460835|qid=Q460835|1=Marugame, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|qid=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}.

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|qid=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}} {{ill|lt=Uta District|Q11173974|qid=Q11173974|1=Utari district}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine stands on the southwestern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mount Iino|Q11666715|qid=Q11666715|1=Mount Iino}}, known as “{{ill|lt=Sanuki Seven Fujis|Q11632541|qid=Q11632541|lt=Sanuki Fuji|1=Seven Sanuki Fujis}},” and it is said that Mount Iino itself is regarded as a {{ill|lt=shintai mountain|Q1595289|qid=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}} <ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Records, Vol. 2 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849/1/68?keyword=%E9%A3%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2025-06-30 |publisher=National Diet Library |author=Kagawa Prefecture Association of Shinto Priests |editor=Kagawa Prefecture Association of Shinto Priests |language=ja |doi=10.11501/1227849}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined Deities ===
=== Principal deities ===
*{{ill|lt=Iii-hiko|Q65292980|qid=Q65292980|1=Iiyorihiko}}
**The {{ill|lt=kunitama deity|Q9692242|qid=Q9692242|lt=land-tutelary deity|1=Kunitama}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|qid=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. One of the aspects of {{ill|lt=Shikoku|Q13991|qid=Q13991|lt=Iyo-no-Futana-shima|1=Shikoku}}. “Iii” is thought to mean “where grains gather,” suggesting a land abundant in food.
*{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}

=== Enshrined together ===
*{{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}
*{{ill|lt=Toshigami|Q3028663|qid=Q3028663|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|1=Toshigami}}

=== History ===
* The founding date is unknown. It is said to have begun in prehistoric times when a group of rocks at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Iino|Q11666715|qid=Q11666715|1=Mount Iino}} were revered as an {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}. After being relocated to the mountainside of Mount Iino, it was moved to its present location in the first year of {{ill|lt=Kōhō|Q1327182|qid=Q1327182|1=Kōhō}} ({{ill|lt=964|Q23951|qid=Q23951|1=964}}) <ref name=":0" />.
* From the second year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|qid=Q30371|1=858}}) to the sixth year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|qid=Q25041|1=864}}), when {{ill|lt=Kinonatsui|Q11605011|qid=Q11605011|1=Kinonatsui}} served as {{ill|lt=Governor of Sanuki Province|Q929682|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki kokushi|1=Sanuki Province}}, devotion to this shrine was strong, and it is said that annual {{ill|lt=heihaku offerings|Q11445508|qid=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} were made.
* From the second year of {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|qid=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} ({{ill|lt=886|Q24683|qid=Q24683|1=886}}) to the sixth year of Ninna ({{ill|lt=890|Q30481|qid=Q30481|1=890}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} served as {{ill|lt=Governor of Sanuki Province|Q929682|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki kokushi|1=Sanuki Province}} and visited the shrine.
* On the 15th day of the 12th month of the fourth year of Ninna ({{ill|lt=888|Q29956|qid=Q29956|1=AD 888}}), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|qid=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} granted the shrine the rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|lt=Junior Fifth Rank Upper|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} in the {{ill|lt=divine court rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} system.
* In the fifth year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it was listed in the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}” and received offerings from the state.
* In the ninth year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1581|Q6717|qid=Q6717|1=1581}}), the shrine was destroyed by fire during the military campaign of {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|qid=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|qid=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}.
* In the eighth year of {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|qid=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} ({{ill|lt=1622|Q6815|qid=Q6815|1=1622}}), the lost shrine buildings were rebuilt.
* In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} of Iino Village.
* In the 22nd year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|qid=Q7831|1=1889}}), the shrine buildings were renovated, resulting in the present structures.
* In the 44th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|qid=Q2076|1=1911}}), Kō Shrine in Takayanagi and {{ill|lt=Aragami Shrine|Q6455190|qid=Q6455190|lt=Aragami Shrine|1=Kōjin}} in Yamane were enshrined together.
* In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|qid=Q2162|1=1921}}), it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} status and designated as a {{ill|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō shinshi|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku}}.
* In the 56th year of {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} ({{ill|lt=1981|Q2437|qid=Q2437|1=1981}}), the shrine buildings were restored.

=== Subsidiary Shrines ===
*Kō Shrine
*Aragami Shrine
*Ise Shrine
*Iitenn Shrine

=== Other ===
When {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} served as {{ill|lt=Governor|Q1351234|qid=Q1351234|1=Kokushi (official)}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|qid=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}, he deeply revered Iii Shrine. It is said that when he returned to the capital, he carved a wooden statue and dedicated it to the shrine. The sacred object of the subsidiary Iitenn Shrine is believed to be that wooden statue <ref name=":0" />.

=== References ===
* Kagawa Prefecture Association of Shinto Priests (ed.), *Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Records*, Vol. 2, Kagawa Prefecture Association of Shinto Priests, 1938. National Diet Library Digital Collection <nowiki>https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849</nowiki> (accessed 2025-06-30)
